name: Current Affairs in History & National Movement (7): Somnath Temple's 1,000-Year Amrut Mahotsav

sourceRefs.label: pmindia.gov.in/indiatvnews.com - Somnath Amrut Mahotsav (marking 1,000 years since the temple's first invasion); ₹75 commemorative coin (gazette notification, 8 May 2026)

researchNotes: Seventh History current-affairs topic (7th lap). A rich combination of medieval Indian history (multiple invasions), the post-Independence reconstruction effort (Sardar Patel, Dr. Rajendra Prasad), and a genuinely dated 2026 commemorative event/coin — exactly the kind of current-affairs item that reinforces core syllabus content rather than sitting disconnected from it.

reading.intro: Covers the Somnath Amrut Mahotsav (2026), marking 1,000 years since the temple's first recorded invasion, and the historical figures behind its modern reconstruction.

qa.question: What milestone did the 'Somnath Amrut Mahotsav' (2026) commemorate?
qa.answer: 1,000 years since the Somnath Temple's first invasion, by Mahmud of Ghazni in 1026 CE.

qa.question: What is Somnath Temple's religious significance?
qa.answer: It is considered the FIRST among the 12 Jyotirlingas of Lord Shiva.

qa.question: Name some historical figures/invasions associated with Somnath Temple's repeated destruction and reconstruction.
qa.answer: Invasions: 1026 CE (Mahmud of Ghazni), 1311, 1326, 1395, 1451 CE, and a destruction order by Aurangzeb in the 17th century. Reconstruction figures: King Kumarapala, Ahilyabai Holkar, and (in modern India) Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel.

qa.question: When was the temple reconsecrated in independent India, and by whom?
qa.answer: 1951, by Dr. Rajendra Prasad, India's first President — Somnath was the very first temple reconstructed in free India.

qa.question: What commemorative coin was released in 2026 to mark this 1,000-year milestone, and what are its specifications?
qa.answer: A ₹75 commemorative coin (gazette notification dated 8 May 2026) — composition: pure silver (99.9%), weight 40 grams, diameter 44 mm, with 200 serrations along the edge. Released by PM Narendra Modi at Veraval, Gujarat, along with a commemorative postal stamp.

questions.prompt: The Somnath Amrut Mahotsav (2026) marked 1,000 years since the temple's first invasion by:
Somnath Temple is considered which Jyotirlinga among the 12 Jyotirlingas of Lord Shiva?
Somnath Temple was reconsecrated in independent India in 1951 by:
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajendra Prasad, India's first President.
